threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think it’s a good idea to queue for hours at a new brunch spot—veterans just pop to the quirky cafes for an instant fix.
Last night, saw a guy on the 333 serenading everyone with a ukulele. Absolutely no one was impressed, but he kept at it till we hit Tulse Hill.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw my neighbor feeding the stray cats while arguing with the pigeons. Only in Neukölln do you find that kind of daily drama.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin can you hop on a tram, get stuck in a spontaneous dance party at the stop, then head to a hidden bar with no sign and amazing cocktails.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s from here if they’ve got a regular burrito order at La Taqueria but still complain about the bus delays like it’s the end of the world.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a Muni bus today that stopped for a dog lounging right on the tracks. The driver just sighed and said, 'Only in San Francisco.'

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think ‘Vermut’ is just a drink; locals know it’s a whole social ritual, often spilling into afternoon siestas and lively debates.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell a new arrival by their bewildered expressions at the Gràcia metro. They stare at the ticket machines like they're space alien tech.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look baffled when a bus driver shouts 'next stop' instead of just stopping. Classic 'not from around here' move.
Last night, caught a couple arguing over who owes who for a takeaway. By the time we hit Herne Hill, they were sharing a half-eaten burger. Classic South London.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a whole family of ducks waddling down the street while the tram was on strike. Only in Neukölln does nature and chaos coexist like that.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain can you find a 'silent disco' in the park where everyone dances to their own beat—headphones on, world off.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s a local if they instinctively know to avoid the BART on Fridays at 5, like it’s a rite of passage. Those train delays are practically a tradition.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 31 last night, and it stopped mid-route for a saxophonist playing 'Take Five.' Only in SF would a Muni delay become an impromptu concert.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists never notice how the 10am chatter in the plaça keeps going as if it’s a cup of café amb llet; life doesn’t pause for siesta here.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by the way they navigate the metro map—head tilted, phone out, still trying to figure out the diagonal line.
